Create a file named `github-instructions.md`.

This file will define coding rules for this project so future code generated by Copilot follows the same architecture.

The instructions should include:

Project JavaScript architecture:

/js
/pages
/components
/services
/utils

Architecture rules:

1. Pages

- Located in /js/pages
- Each HTML page should import one page script
- Pages coordinate data fetching and UI rendering

Example:
productDetailPage.js
productListPage.js

2. Components

- Located in /js/components
- Responsible only for rendering UI
- Must NOT fetch data or call APIs
- Must be reusable

Example:
productCard.js
reviewItem.js
cartItem.js

3. Services

- Located in /js/services
- Responsible for fetching or simulating API calls
- Must NOT manipulate the DOM
- Must return Promises

Example:
productService.js
categoryService.js
reviewService.js

4. Utils

- Located in /js/utils
- Shared helper functions
- DOM helpers
- Data utilities

5. Coding rules

- Use ES modules
- Do not attach objects to window
- Keep UI logic separated from data logic
- Page files orchestrate services and components

6. Future development

When adding new features:

- Add API logic in services
- Add UI rendering in components
- Coordinate everything in pages

The goal is to keep the codebase modular, maintainable, and easy to scale.

7. CSS and JavaScript classes must be separated.

Any class used by JavaScript must start with the prefix js-

Styling classes must NOT be used for JavaScript selectors

JavaScript should only query elements using classes that start with js-

Example:

HTML

<button class="btn btn-primary js-add-to-cart">
Add to cart
</button>

CSS

.btn {
padding: 12px;
}

JavaScript

document.querySelector(".js-add-to-cart")

1. Use semantic tags (header, section, main, footer)
2. Only one h1 per page
3. Avoid unnecessary div nesting
4. Use ul/li for lists
5. Images must have alt
6. Use figure for images with captions
7. Use a for navigation actions

CSS

1. Do not set fixed width/height on body
2. Use margin for spacing between blocks
3. Use padding for inner spacing
4. Use flexbox with display:flex
5. Use gap instead of margin for flex spacing
6. Line-height should be between 1 and 1.5
7. Avoid inline CSS
8. Use SVG for icons

JS

1. Use js- prefix for JS hooks
2. Separate CSS class and JS class
3. Render dynamic data via JS
4. Use English comments only
